baer_hughes_sbs.description: BAER Soil Burn Severity raster, Hughes fire (Jan 2025)
baer_hughes_sbs.provenance_note: File carries no metadata beyond DataType=Generic / AREA_OR_POINT=Area. Cannot be checksum-matched against a specific BAER publication. Class coding was established by inspecting spatial structure and per-class Tanager NBR, not from documentation. See data/reference/baer/README.md.

globe_lfmc_xlsx.provenance_note: Published dataset with DOI; checksum taken from the local copy.

globe_lfmc_csv.provenance_note: CSV conversion of the xlsx; see globe_lfmc_xlsx for the authoritative source.

dins_palisades.description: CAL FIRE DINS structure-damage survey, Palisades fire (Jan 2025)
dins_palisades.provenance_note: Published by CAL FIRE per incident via the DINS viewer.

aviris3_reflectance.description: AVIRIS-3 L2A surface-reflectance NetCDF granules, Jan 23 2025 Palisades overpass
aviris3_reflectance.provenance_note: ORNL DAAC archive with DOI. Granule URLs listed in data/raw/aviris3/aviris3_jan23_palisades_urls.txt.

tanager_scenes.description: Tanager-1 ortho_sr HDF5 scenes from the Planet Tanager Open Data Competition
tanager_scenes.license: Planet Tanager Open Data Competition terms
tanager_scenes.provenance_note: Retrieved via Planet STAC catalog. Scenes are too large to bundle (~1.3 GB each).

nb02_hughes_kappa.section: 7. Validation against BAER ground truth (5-class, code 4 kept separate)
nb02_hughes_kappa.description: Cohen's kappa for RF severity classifier on Hughes BAER SBS — provisional
nb02_hughes_kappa.independence_note: Provisional against an unverifiable reference. (1) BAER SBS class coding was inferred from spatial structure and per-class Tanager NBR (partly the quantity being predicted), not from documentation. (2) The raster's identity is unverified: per-class proportions are near-inverted vs the published USDA BAER Hughes summary. (3) Temporal mismatch: the Hughes swath (2025-01-23) is ~1 day post-ignition with the fire still burning, while BAER SBS is mapped after containment. (4) Code 4 (High, ~141 px) is now kept as its own class rather than merged into code 3, so the kappa changes from the prior 4-class value of 0.527 and must be recomputed on notebook re-execution.

nb02_dins_spearman.description: Spearman rho between dNBR and DINS structure damage ordinal
nb02_dins_spearman.independence_note: DINS is a field-collected, structure-level reference entirely independent of Tanager imagery.

nb03_lfmc_data_availability.description: Count of Globe-LFMC 2.0 field sites colocated with the scene footprint — assessment of whether a calibrated LFMC regression can be trained at all
nb03_lfmc_data_availability.independence_note: Pure counting exercise: how many Globe-LFMC sites fall on valid scene pixels. No model is trained, so it cannot be circular. The count is ~1 site per footprint (area ratio: ~60 SoCal sites over 230x165 km vs an ~18-30 km swath), and the SoCal record ends 2023-01-30 while scenes are Dec 2024-Apr 2025 — far below the >=10 sites a full-spectrum PLSR needs. NB03 therefore ships spectral water indices, not a calibrated LFMC percent. The prior synthetic PLSR (inject_water_absorption, cv_r2=0.904) and its LFMC map / fire-danger overlay were removed.

nb05_cross_sensor_char.description: Cross-sensor MESMA char fraction comparison (Tanager vs simulated EMIT/PRISMA)
nb05_cross_sensor_char.independence_note: Both sensors are simulated from the same Tanager reflectance cube via spectral degradation. This compares information loss, not independent measurements.

globe_lfmc_socal_shrub_monthly_median.note: A predicted LFMC mean outside [median / tolerance_factor, median * tolerance_factor] for the scene month is implausible and should fail the gate.
